fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Presenting dcpos and dcpo algebras
fr:frontmatter.fr:meta: @article{jung-moshier-vickers-2008, author = {Achim Jung and M. Andrew Moshier and Steve Vickers}, doi = {10.1016/j.entcs.2008.10.013}, issn = {1571-0661}, journal = {Electronic Notes in Theoretical Computer Science}, keywords = {Dcpo algebras, dcpo presentations, d-topology, -topology, coverage theorems}, note = {Proceedings of the 24th Conference on the Mathematical Foundations of Programming Semantics (MFPS XXIV)}, pages = {209-229}, title = {Presenting Dcpos and Dcpo Algebras}, volume = {218}, year = {2008}, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Dcpos can be presented by preorders of generators and inequational relations expressed as covers. Algebraic operations on the generators (possibly with their results being ideals of generators) can be extended to the dcpo presented, provided the covers are “stable” for the operations. The resulting dcpo algebra has a natural universal characterization and satisfies all the inequational laws satisfied by the generating algebra. Applications include known “coverage theorems” from locale theory.

fr:title.text: A new characterization of lambda definability

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: A new characterization of lambda definability

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We give a new characterization of lambda definability in Henkin models using logical relations defined over ordered sets with varying arity. The advantage of this over earlier approaches by Plotkin and Statman is its simplicity and universality. Yet, decidability of lambda definability for hereditarily finite Henkin models remains an open problem. But if the variable set allowed in terms is also restricted to be finite then our techniques lead to a decision procedure.
